strategy: agentic chunking via Gemini (headline + summary + original text)

pages.text:
Bayesian Bodybuilding 2014
1

Schedule
 Your first meal of the day should be when you naturally get hungry on most days.
This is usually a few hours after waking up. Don't forcefeed your breakfast, but don't
go hungry either.
 Keep your daily meal frequency constant: always eat 4 meals at roughly the same
times every day.
 It is important to always train at roughly the same time, so find a time to train when
you are sure that you can train in between your shifts. If possible, it's better to train
at a time when you are always already awake for several hours (so not in the
morning).
 Feeding and training windows of 2 hours are acceptable, so you don't have to obsess
over the timing.
 See your diet spreadsheet for your body composition tracking. The bulk, cut and
training days are just an example. Fill in your own training days based on your own
training program.

Bayesian Bodybuilding 2014
2

Diet
You do not have a typical 'diet'. You will also not track your macros or count your
calories. Instead, we are going to get you to eat healthily and consistently and turn this
eating pattern into a sustainable lifestyle. We will start by including only foods that are
low in FODMAPs and may cause digestive issues or symptoms of food intolerance.
Your training days are bulk days and your rest days are cut days.

Cut days
On cutting days your food choices are restricted to
1) Non-dairy protein sources (e.g. fish, meat, poultry) with a maximum ratio of 1:2 carb:
protein and a 1:4 fat:protein ratio. In other words, something with 20 g protein per
100 g needs to have less than 10 (=20/2) grams of carbs and less than 5 (=20/4)
grams of fats.
2) Low FODMAP, nonstarchy vegetables: green beans, carrots, cauliflower, celery,
chilies, cucumber, greens (collard greens, kale, mustard greens, turnip greens), jicama,
leeks, lettuce (endive, escarole, iceberg lettuce, leafy varieties, radicchio, romaine),
parsley, parsnips, peppers, pumpkin, radishes, rutabaga, scallions, spinach, squash,
Swiss chard, tomato, turnips and zucchini.
3) All fermented vegetables: Sauerkraut (preferably raw and unpasteurized), kimchi,
pickles, curtido, beet kvass, kombucha, water kefir, coconut kefir, etc.
4) Low calorie, low FODMAP fruits: berries (all except blackberries), papaya,
cantaloupe melon and grapefruit.

Bayesian Bodybuilding 2014
3

5) Vinegar, extra virgine olive oil, mustard, low calorie condiments, herbs and spices and
anything with zero calories and no caffeine.
6) Bone broth (highly recommended for gut health and satiety).
Every meal should contain a minimum of 25 g protein.
No protein powder and no calories in beverage form. Decaffeinated or herbal tea and
decaffeinated coffee is highly recommended, as are carbonated diet sodas.
Don't forget to take your l-tryptophan along with every meal.

Bulk days
Your first 2 meals follow the same guidelines as the cut day meals. You can also eat 1 piece
(10 g) of >= 85% dark chocolate with one of these meals unless you cannot resist eating the
whole thing afterwards.
During meals 3 and 4, you can also eat the following foods.
 Low FODMAP fruits: grapes, oranges, cantaloupe melon, honeydew melon and
peaches.
 Nonstarchy, non-dairy, fermented foods: kefir, natto, tempeh and miso.
 Unprocessed poultry (all kinds).
 Unprocessed meats (all kinds).
 Unprocessed fish (all kinds).
 Coconut.
 Olives.
During meal 4, you can also eat the following foods.

Bayesian Bodybuilding 2014
4

 Low FODMAP or fermented starches: White rice (all kinds), yams, (sweet) potatoes,
tapioca, yucca/cassava/manioc, taro, lotus root, plantains, rutabagas and breadfruit.
Think about how your meals would look and, if you want, send me some examples and I'll
advise you on any beneficial adjustments. The diet will require some behavioral change, but
in 2 weeks you should be fully used to it and you shouldn't feel like you're really dieting.

If you run into any difficulty whatsoever, be it hunger, fatigue, lack of preparation
or anything else, I cannot help you if you don't communicate it.
